channel.title: KeepassXC Broken Functionality (Wayland Issue?)
channel.description:
On KeepassXC in the GUI the preview panel doesn’t show despite it being enabled?
> Kicksecure 18

item.title: KeepassXC Broken Functionality (Wayland Issue?)
item.dc:creator: anon92632958
item.description:
arraybolt3:

It’s there, but needs to be expanded. If you look at the bottom of the window, you’ll see, right underneath the password table, a very small horizontal line. If you hover your mouse over that, it will turn into a “slider” icon. If you click and drag upward at that point, the preview pane should pop open. You have to drag up pretty far before it pops out.

I thought there was more then 3 algo options available in Keepassxc? Maybe enabled by other plugins? Maybe a case of “Bernstein bears”, where I remembered it to be one way but actually it wasn’t ever that way.

Read full topic

item.title: KeepassXC Broken Functionality (Wayland Issue?)
item.dc:creator: anon92632958
item.description:
arraybolt3:

It’s there, but needs to be expanded. If you look at the bottom of the window, you’ll see, right underneath the password table, a very small horizontal line. If you hover your mouse over that, it will turn into a “slider” icon. If you click and drag upward at that point, the preview pane should pop open. You have to drag up pretty far before it pops out.

Thank you so much, that’s exactly what I was trying to fix. What made me open this initial thread was my experience on other systems was different. On Debian GNOME with wayland it sizes right and I can see it. On KDE with/without Wayland it sizes right. Only distro w Wayland that is using LXQt that I tried was Kicksecure. This was not in a VM nor was tried w the others.

More then likely its a bug related to Wayland or dracut, though I would say the latter. Wayland has documented issues with window sizing, resizing, and dragging, particularly affecting XWayland apps.

Here is a related issue where a user is using Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition). Two things from just that alone that seem related here. Fedora Linux 42 Workstation Edition uses Wayland and Dracut. Both in which Kicksecure uses despite being it using Debian.

github.com/keepassxreboot/keepassxc

Preview panel occasionally gets collapsed

opened 10:01AM - 26 Apr 25 UTC
michaelkuhn
user interface not reproducible

### Have you searched for an existing issue? - [x] Yes, I tried searching and r…eviewed the pinned issues ### Brief Summary Sometimes my preview panel gets collapsed without me touching it. The panel is visible in KeePassXC, but when closing the application, it gets collapsed in the cache file (`PreviewSplitterState=252, 306` changes to `PreviewSplitterState=504, 0`). When opening KeePassXC again, it is collapsed and needs to be resized to be visible. ### Steps to Reproduce I have not been able to find exact reproduction steps so far. Simply unlocking a database, moving the window around (without modifying the preview panel's size) and closing the application is enough to sometimes trigger this. ### Expected Versus Actual Behavior The preview panel should not change its size and/or state on its own. ### KeePassXC Debug Information ```Text KeePassXC - Version 2.7.10 Revision: b342be4 Qt 5.15.16 Debugging mode is disabled. Operating system: Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition) CPU architecture: x86_64 Kernel: linux 6.14.2-300.fc42.x86_64 Enabled extensions: - Auto-Type - Browser Integration - Passkeys - SSH Agent - KeeShare - YubiKey - Secret Service Integration Cryptographic libraries: - Botan 2.19.5 ``` ### Operating System Linux ### Linux Desktop Environment Gnome ### Linux Windowing System Wayland

Read full topic

item.title: KeepassXC Broken Functionality (Wayland Issue?)
item.dc:creator: arraybolt3
item.description:
anon92632958:

On KeepassXC in the GUI the preview panel doesn’t show despite it being enabled?

It’s there, but needs to be expanded. If you look at the bottom of the window, you’ll see, right underneath the password table, a very small horizontal line. If you hover your mouse over that, it will turn into a “slider” icon. If you click and drag upward at that point, the preview pane should pop open. You have to drag up pretty far before it pops out.

anon92632958:

Also not sure if I’m hallucinating but I could have sworn I only saw AES-256, no ChaCha or TwoFish cipher algo options when creating a new database?

I see ChaCha and TwoFish options on my end.

Read full topic

item.title: KeepassXC Broken Functionality (Wayland Issue?)
item.dc:creator: anon92632958
item.description:
Also not sure if I’m hallucinating but I could have sworn I only saw AES-256, no ChaCha or TwoFish cipher algo options when creating a new database?

Read full topic

item.title: KeepassXC Broken Functionality (Wayland Issue?)
item.dc:creator: anon92632958
item.description:
On KeepassXC in the GUI the preview panel doesn’t show despite it being enabled?
